title: Conjecture 1 (Gyárfás–Sumner)
statement_text: For every tree $H$, the class of $H$-free graphs is $\chi$-bounded.
context_text: One of the central problems regarding $\chi$-boundedness, formulated independently by Gyárfás and Sumner. The conjecture is resolved only for very specific trees $H$; it is open in particular for $P_t$-free graphs with $t \geq 5$, and it is unknown even whether $P_5$-free graphs are polynomially $\chi$-bounded.
paper_contribution: Proves a biclique-exclusion relaxation: every $C_{\geq t}$-free graph containing no $K_{\ell,\ell}$ as a subgraph satisfies $\chi(G) \leq \ell^{f(t)}+1$, giving a polynomial bound in $\ell$ under the additional assumption of no large balanced biclique.
paper_title: Degeneracy of $P_t$-free and $C_{\geq t}$-free graphs with no large complete bipartite subgraphs

title: Conjecture 8 (Erdős–Hajnal)
statement_text: For every graph $H$, there exists $\varepsilon_H > 0$ such that every $H$-free graph contains an independent set of size at least $n^{\varepsilon_H}$ or a clique of size at least $n^{\varepsilon_H}$.
context_text: The Erdős–Hajnal conjecture can be seen as a weakening of polynomial $\chi$-boundedness; it is known to hold for cographs, graphs on at most four vertices, the bull graph, and $C_5$, but remains open for $P_5$-free graphs. The paper discusses it under Connection 2 as motivation for a biclique variant.
paper_contribution: Presents Theorem 4 (degeneracy of $C_{\geq t}$-free graphs excluding $K_{\ell,\ell}$) as the $C_{\geq t}$-free analogue of the Fox–Sudakov biclique version of the conjecture, drawing an explicit structural parallel between the two settings.
paper_title: Degeneracy of $P_t$-free and $C_{\geq t}$-free graphs with no large complete bipartite subgraphs
